title: PAP AST v0.1
description: Patterns-as-Primary expression AST. A PAP document is a set of named symbols (frames, terms, cells, bonds), expressions composed from them, projections (rotations into a frame), renders (emit to a target), receipts (world-contact evidence), and assertions (checkable properties). emit and ingest are inverses over THIS object: emit walks it to a target format; ingest parses a target format back into it. coherency is COMPUTED by passes WF1-WF5; grounding is GATED by WF6.

frames.description: Named vantage points. A frame is a (substrate, lens) origin. Every non-invariant expression names one.

substrate.description: Carrier domain / library namespace, e.g. physics, cryptography.

lens.description: The observer projection within the substrate, e.g. claude_code.

terms.description: Non-cell library symbols imported from a shared library (e.g. a shared enum or named constant). The stdlib symbols that are not cells.

cells.description: (pattern x substrate) instances. The leaf unit the proof_index keys on. `trust` is RESOLVED from proof_index at check time; an authored value is a claim WF1 verifies.

pattern.description: A pattern identifier or a descriptive pattern name.

trust.description: Resolved trust status. NEGATIVE cells are refused by WF2.

proof_ref.description: proof_id (or candidate id) backing the trust status. A bare id.

preconditions.description: For CONDITIONAL cells: preconditions that must hold.

claim.description: OPTIONAL authored assertion carried by a CLAIM cell. `text` is the prose the renderer emits; `register` is the rhetorical register the prose claims. WF5 (claim-license) checks register <= the license the cited proof_ref grants: a PROVED structural/bridge proof licenses up to `structural`; only a substrate-Faithful PROVED licenses `substrate`; `empirical` is licensed by NO proof (it requires a fresh world-contact receipt, WF6) — so an empirical register over a Lean citation is the canonical over-claim WF5 rejects (a proof does not license a world-measurement). coherency (computed) and register (audited) are independent: a claim can rest on a PROVED cell yet still over-claim in prose.

text.description: The authored assertion prose (may contain newlines).

register.description: neutral < analogy < structural < substrate < empirical. The strongest register the prose claims. WF5 rejects any register exceeding the cited proof_ref's license.

layout.description: OPTIONAL renderer configuration carried by a layout cell (not used by these examples).

lean.description: OPTIONAL real, buildable Lean carried by a proof-bearing cell: import/open/theorem statement + a real proof term (NOT `sorry`) whose proof delegates to the cited theorem, so the emitted fragment builds clean.

imports.description: Lake module imports for the emitted fragment (e.g. GateProofs).

opens.description: `open` namespaces the emitted fragment needs (e.g. PAPGate).

statement.description: The theorem statement (real Lean, e.g. `accepts goodReceipt liveSha = true`).

proof.description: The real proof tactic/term (NOT `sorry`; e.g. `by decide`, or `exact <thm>` delegating to a cited theorem).

bonds.description: Proven structural relationships that LICENSE a composition. The typed connective between cells.

receipts.description: World-contact evidence. Attaching a fresh receipt is the ONLY way an expression's grounding upgrades above floating (WF6). Cannot be synthesized from coherent self-reference. For a COMPUTATIONAL world-contact (a Lean build of the cited proof), the receipt carries the grounding-ledger fields below — additive + optional. WF6's ACCEPT branch is gated on (verdict==GROUNDED ∧ executed ∧ build_exit==0) AND a REAL recency check: source_sha256 must equal the live cited .lean's current sha256 (a content-predicate freshness check — a stale/sha-drifted receipt is REJECTED). Freshness is NOT mere presence of a stamp string.

axis.description: reality_contact axis level (R0 = no world-contact / structural; higher = stronger observational anchor). R1 = ONE concrete external world-contact — computational/executed (e.g. a lake build) OR observational/social (e.g. a witnessed external adoption). The KIND of contact is carried orthogonally by `mechanism`, not by the axis level.

source.description: What the observation/experiment/dataset is (e.g. 'Lean build of GateProofs.lean').

witness.description: Who/what witnessed it (e.g. a cross-checker session id, or a fresh-build identifier).

freshness.description: ISO timestamp the receipt was witnessed (human-readable). The OPERATIVE recency check is the content predicate source_sha256==live (WF6), not this stamp; the stamp is for the rendered receipt line.

mechanism.description: How the entry was world-grounded. Computational (kernel) mechanisms: lake_build / lake_env_lean_standalone / prover_corroboration. Observational/social mechanism: witnessed_adoption — an independent party ADOPTED the work, witnessed by an acknowledgement + the adopter’s persisting referencing edit. For witnessed_adoption, WF6 freshness is the social analog of source_sha256: the adopter artifact (`adopter_ref`) must STILL contain `citation_token` at check time (un-adoption -> STALE -> REJECT).

verdict.description: The grounding verdict. WF6 accepts ONLY GROUNDED; DIVERGENT/UNVERIFIABLE keep the expression floating (the honest finding).

executed.description: Whether the build was actually executed (the anti-laundering guard: an inferred-not-run verdict is UNVERIFIABLE, never GROUNDED).

build_exit.description: The executed build's exit code. WF6 requires 0; a non-compiling build (exit != 0) is REJECTED.

lean_file.description: The cited .lean path relative to lean_root (e.g. GateProofs.lean). WF6 hashes the LIVE file here for the recency check.

source_sha256.description: sha256 of the cited .lean at build time. FRESH iff it equals the live file's current sha256 (content-predicate staleness).

mathlib_rev.description: Pinned mathlib manifest rev at build time; FRESH requires it match the live manifest (Mathlib drift stales a receipt even with the .lean unchanged).

lean_toolchain.description: Pinned lean toolchain at build time; FRESH requires it match the live lean-toolchain.

witnessed_utc.description: ISO UTC stamp of the executed build.

independence.description: Witness independence level (e.g. N=2 cross-vendor, or N=1.5_same_vendor_fresh_build).

built_module.description: The lake module name that was built (e.g. GateProofs). Rendered into a GROUNDED `lean` fragment's world-contact comment line.

emitted_module.description: The lake module name the EMITTED `lean` theorem itself was built as (the emitted theorem builds clean). Corroborating, not the WF6 freshness target.

emitted_build_exit.description: The emitted theorem's own `lake build` exit code (0 = the emitted fragment compiles against the Lean kernel).

emitted_source_sha256.description: sha256 of the exact bytes produced for the emitted (built) theorem; preserved for reproduction (the world-contact line embeds the CITED file's sha, not this one — non-circular).

prover.description: The independent prover that re-proved the cited statement. Present on a `prover_corroboration` receipt; the WF6c gate REJECTS such a receipt if it lacks a prover or an accepted prover_verdict. Corroboration only — NOT a stronger grounding tier.

prover_verdict.description: The independent prover's verdict on the cited statement. WF6c accepts ONLY a re-proved verdict with sorry_count 0; a corroboration receipt with no prover_verdict is REJECTED.

status.description: re-proved (the prover closed the sorry'd statement from scratch) or a non-accepted status.

sorry_count.description: remaining sorries in the prover's solution; must be 0 to corroborate.

project_id.description: The prover run's project/job id; the corroboration line embeds its first 12 chars.

prover_lineage.description: The prover's proof lineage — names WHICH independent prover corroborates, so cross-prover breadth is auditable.

corroborates.description: What this corroboration backs (the grounding receipt name or the cited statement) — provenance, not a grounding upgrade.

adopter_ref.description: witnessed_adoption: the adopter artifact (path under the project root). The re-checkable freshness SUBSTRATE — WF6 re-reads it and confirms it still contains `citation_token`. The social analog of `lean_file`.

citation_token.description: witnessed_adoption: the stable token the adopter must cite (e.g. the coupling id). FRESH iff `adopter_ref` still contains this string at check time; absent (un-adopted) -> STALE -> WF6 REJECT. The social analog of `source_sha256` content-freshness.

witness_ref.description: witnessed_adoption: the id of the adoption acknowledgement (the witness EVENT, for provenance/audit).

adopter_source_sha256.description: witnessed_adoption: sha256 of `adopter_ref` at witness time (provenance snapshot; NOT the staling predicate — only removing `citation_token` stales the adoption).

adopter_count.description: witnessed_adoption: number of DISTINCT independent adopters (Condition C, honest N). One acknowledgement + the same adopter's edit is ONE adopter (N=1). WF6 over-claim guard: axis above R1 requires adopter_count >= 2.

adopters.description: witnessed_adoption: the DISTINCT adopter identities. len(adopters) must equal adopter_count (no double-counting one adopter via ack+edit).

expressions.description: The core node. compose(symbols) via bonds @ frame :: orientation.

kind.description: invariant = frame-free (satisfies WF3 vacuously); frame_bound = must name a frame.

frame.description: Reference into `frames`. null IFF kind == invariant.

compose.description: References into cells, terms, or other expressions. The structure being composed.

via.description: References into `bonds` that license the composition. Empty allowed for invariants / single-symbol exprs.

primary.description: OPTIONAL frame-relative primacy claim (which composed symbol is primary FROM THIS FRAME). WF3 REJECTS any global/frameless primacy assertion; primacy here is valid only because it is scoped to `frame`.

coherency.description: COMPUTED by WF1-WF4 from composed cells/bonds. An authored value is checked, not trusted.

status.description: floating = coherent but no observational anchor (city-in-the-sky; LEGAL, must be labeled). grounded = fresh receipt attached (WF6).

receipt.description: Reference into `receipts`. Required when status == grounded.

corroborations.description: OPTIONAL references into `receipts` to independent-prover CORROBORATION receipts (mechanism prover_corroboration). A corroboration is an external re-prove of the SAME statement — cross-prover breadth, NOT a stronger grounding tier; it does NOT change `axis`, which is set by the grounding `receipt` alone. The WF6c gate validates each referenced corroboration carries an accepted prover verdict.

projections.description: A rotation of an expression into a frame. Projection is the morphism on the toroidal manifold of frames; no projection is privileged.

renders.description: emit to a target format. The writer direction. (ingest is the inverse: target -> AST.)

assertions.description: Checkable properties over the document (e.g. rotation-invariance within a projection class = a renderer's byte-identical guarantee).

$comment: Well-formedness passes (the checker; not enforced by this JSON Schema, run separately over a valid document): WF1 resolve every cell.proof_ref in the proof register. WF2 refuse any NEGATIVE cell. WF3 every frame_bound expression names a frame and asserts no GLOBAL primacy (frame-relative `primary` only). WF4 the compose/via graph is acyclic OR every cycle passes through a receipt-bearing (grounding) node = open-mouth. WF5 prose-register <= cited license (claim-license check). WF6 grounding.status==grounded requires a receipt that is (verdict==GROUNDED ∧ executed ∧ build_exit==0) AND FRESH — its source_sha256 equals the live cited .lean's current sha256 (content-predicate recency; a stale/sha-drifted or non-compiling receipt is REJECTED). coherency is the WF1-WF4 result; grounding is the WF6 gate.
